description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a striped pattern of alternating light and dark bands. The grooves are deep and regular, giving the block a visually striking, ribbed appearance. The sides of the block are solid and unadorned, with a dark, almost black coloration that contrasts sharply with the lighter, stone-colored top surface. The block appears dense and heavy, likely made from a durable stone material such as granite or concrete. Its design suggests it could serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, possibly for traffic control or as a decorative element in public spaces. There are no visible signs of weathering, engravings, or additional features beyond the grooved top.
description: A heavy, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its light-colored top and solid dark sides, creating a bold striped pattern. The block is dense and visually striking, suitable for use as an urban barrier or obstacle.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block with horizontal grooves and a solid dark base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the block's rectangular profile.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the striped top and solid dark side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the deep grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative or functional barrier
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: no visible weathering or erosion; surfaces appear clean and well-maintained
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
